canonical_source_tree: book_structure.json plus canonical chapter, source, evidence, proof, and release records

products.label: Narrative technical book
products.reader_question: What is the architecture, why does it matter, and how do its pieces fit together?
products.navigation_contract: Part and chapter progression, Human Reading Path bridges, summaries, handoffs, glossary, and selected source appendices.
products.density_contract: Hide live status, test, proof-hook, source-crosswalk, and repeated support boilerplate; retain meaning-changing uncertainty and plain-language evidence boundaries.
products.review_gate: Chapter continuity, editorial review, format-specific review, accessibility, figures, and an edition release record.
products.forbidden_inference: Human view convenience does not mean a reviewed reader artifact exists.

products.label: Architecture reference specification
products.primary_audience: system designers, implementers, and technical reviewers
products.reader_question: What are the responsibilities, interfaces, states, invariants, failure routes, and implementation horizons?
products.navigation_contract: Layer and interface lookup through the AI/research view, integrated reference trace, protocol schemas, implementation horizons, and glossary.
products.density_contract: Retain mechanisms, interfaces, invariants, failure modes, formal hooks, test plans, schemas, and implementation horizons; route evidence totals to the registry instead of repeating them as architecture prose.
products.review_gate: Interface consistency, schema/examples, trace coverage, proof-scope review, implementation-horizon review, and a research release record.
products.forbidden_inference: A complete reference record does not establish deployed enforcement or system quality.

products.label: Evidence, proof, and release registry
products.primary_audience: auditors, reviewers, researchers, and maintenance agents
products.reader_question: What is actually supported, by which source or artifact, under what scope, and with which residuals?
products.navigation_contract: Canonical status first, then claim/evidence matrix, source ownership appendices, test/proof ledgers, release records, changelog, and residual-specific reports.
products.density_contract: Prefer structured records, exact IDs, digests, commands, support-state effects, negative controls, and non-claims over narrative repetition.
products.review_gate: Schema and ledger validation, source ownership, artifact/reality checks, clean commit-bound status, deployment attestation, and independent review where claimed.
products.forbidden_inference: Record count, schema validity, or validator success does not prove the underlying architecture or chapter claims.

shared_rules: All three products derive from one canonical source tree; none may become an untracked parallel manuscript or evidence ledger.
A product projection may omit detail but may not omit uncertainty that changes claim meaning.
Product availability, artifact approval, and support-state evidence are separate states.
Navigation labels must identify the product question instead of presenting all technical surfaces as one reading path.
No product contract promotes a chapter-core support state.
